The resin of some Commiphora species, such as Commiphora molmol, has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce fever, relieve pain, and aid in wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Commiphora resin include terpenes, such as α-bisabolol, and fatty acids, such as oleic acid. Commiphora resin is often used to make herbal preparations, including ointments, salves, and tinctures, which are used topically to treat various skin conditions and injuries.
